description: university in Stockholm, Sweden
independent school in Singapore, Singapore
consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

aliases: Great San Francisco Earthquake and Fire
description: major earthquake that struck San Francisco and the coast of Northern California
description_wiki: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake was a major earthquake which struck the coast of Northern California at 05:12 am Pacific Standard Time on Wednesday, April 18, 1906.

description: place in California listed on National Register of Historic Places
description_wiki: The Presidio of San Francisco (originally, El Presidio Real de San Francisco or The Royal Fortress of Saint Francis) is a park and former U.S. Army post on the northern tip of the San Francisco Peninsula in San Francisco, California, and is part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area.

description: American company which manufactured railroad cars
description_wiki: The Pullman Company, founded by George Pullman, was a manufacturer of railroad cars in the mid-to-late 19th century through the first half of the 20th century, during the boom of railroads in the United States.

description: first railroad in the United States to reach the Pacific coast from the eastern states
capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: city in Solano County, California, United States
description_wiki: Vallejo ( və-LAY-(h)oh; Spanish pronunciation: [baˈʎexo] ) is a city in Solano County, California, United States, and the second largest city in the North Bay region of the Bay Area.

description: 1905-1997 shipbuilding company in the United States
description_wiki: Bethlehem Steel Corporation Shipbuilding Division was created in 1905 when the Bethlehem Steel Corporation of Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, acquired the San Francisco-based shipyard Union Iron Works.

description: shipbuilding firm in San Francisco
description_wiki: Union Iron Works, located in San Francisco, California, on the southeast waterfront, was a central business within the large industrial zone of Potrero Point, for four decades at the end of the nineteenth and beginning of the twentieth centuries.

description: United States Class I railroad (1865–1996)
provider of fire and emergency medical services to the City and County of San Francisco, California
description_wiki: The San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD) provides firefighting, hazardous materials response services, technical rescue services and emergency medical response services to the City and County of San Francisco, California.

description: African American Branch of Freemasonry
description_wiki: Prince Hall Freemasonry is a branch of North American Freemasonry created for African Americans, founded by Prince Hall on September 29, 1784.

aliases: United States Navy Yard, New York
description: shipyard located in Brooklyn, New York
description_wiki: The Brooklyn Navy Yard (originally known as the New York Navy Yard) is a shipyard and industrial complex in northwest Brooklyn in New York City, New York, U.S. The Navy Yard is located on the East River in Wallabout Bay, a semicircular bend of the river across from Corlears Hook in Manhattan.

entity: Cooper African Methodist Episcopal Zion Church
description: predominantly African American religious organization
description_wiki: The African Methodist Episcopal Zion Church, or the AME Zion Church (AMEZ) is a historically African-American Christian denomination based in the United States.

description: spiritual organization
description_wiki: Unity is a spiritual organization founded by Charles and Myrtle Fillmore in 1889.

description: large defunct saltwater swimming pool complex in San Francisco
description_wiki: The Sutro Baths was a large, privately owned public saltwater swimming pool complex in the Lands End area of the Outer Richmond District on the West Side of San Francisco, California.

description: 20th-century American politician (1922–2016)
president of the United States from 1981 to 1989 and actor (1911–2004)
description_wiki: Ronald Wilson Reagan (February 6, 1911 – June 5, 2004) was an American politician and actor who served as the 40th president of the United States from 1981 to 1989.
